established.statement: The dataset now combines 80 observations from 1901, 83 from 1911, and 69 from 1926 into one analyzable corpus.
21 family lines in the current analysis reach the first Irish Free State census in 1926.
The strongest place concentration in the current corpus is Limerick | Glenbrohane | Ballyfroota.
Martin Quish line at Limerick | Duntryleague | Duntryleague forms a multi-census line across 1901, 1911, 1926.
Patrick Quish line at Limerick | Glenbrohane | Knockaunavlyman forms a multi-census line across 1901, 1911, 1926.
Patrick Quish line at Limerick | Ballylanders | Killeen forms a multi-census line across 1901, 1911, 1926.
Patrick Quish line at Limerick | Emlygrennan | Cush forms a multi-census line across 1901, 1911, 1926.
Pat Quish line at Limerick | Hospital | Ballycahill forms a multi-census line across 1901, 1911, 1926.
Thomas Quish line at Limerick | Glenbrohane | Ballyfroota forms a multi-census line across 1901, 1911, 1926.
Martin Quish line at Limerick | Glenbrohane | Ballyfroota forms a multi-census line across 1911, 1926.

openQuestions.statement: Single-census appearances and weaker inferred links still need manual genealogical review before they are presented as family continuity.
This analysis establishes census-based continuity evidence, not full kinship proof; church, civil, and land records are still needed for stronger family-tree assertions.
